boundary_evals.prompt:
You are a reviewer agent evaluating a work item through the completeness lens.

Read the completeness lens skill at skills/review/lenses/completeness-lens/SKILL.md fully.

Read the output format specification at:
skills/review/output-formats/work-item-review-output-format/SKILL.md

Read the work item at skills/review/lenses/scope-lens/evals/files/epic-bundling-unrelated-themes/work-item.md fully.

Apply the completeness lens guidance to evaluate the work item. Return your analysis as a single JSON code block following the output format specification. Do not include any prose outside the JSON block.
boundary_evals.expected_output: A JSON block with lens='completeness'. The reviewer produces no finding whose body mentions bundling, sizing, that the work item should be split into separate epics, or that the themes are unrelated. Completeness may flag absent-section observations only (e.g., a section that is missing or empty). No finding at major or higher about scope or decomposition coherence.

boundary_evals.prompt:
You are a reviewer agent evaluating a work item through the completeness lens.

Read the completeness lens skill at skills/review/lenses/completeness-lens/SKILL.md fully.

Read the output format specification at:
skills/review/output-formats/work-item-review-output-format/SKILL.md

Read the work item at skills/review/lenses/dependency-lens/evals/files/implied-external-dep-missing/work-item.md fully.

Apply the completeness lens guidance to evaluate the work item. Return your analysis as a single JSON code block following the output format specification. Do not include any prose outside the JSON block.
boundary_evals.expected_output: A JSON block with lens='completeness'. The reviewer produces no finding whose body alleges that implied Stripe or secrets-management couplings are missing from the content of the Dependencies section. Completeness may flag that the Dependencies section states 'None identified' and that this is potentially sparse for a story involving an external service — but must not reason about which specific couplings are missing from the content. Any finding about the Dependencies section should be about the section's structural completeness (is it present, is it populated at all), not about what implied-but-uncaptured couplings should appear in it.
